---
title: "HW1 Mason Holcombe"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## 1

```{r}
x1 = c(3,4,2,6,8,2,5)
x2 = c(5,5.5,4,7,10,5,7.5)
x1bar = sum(x1) / 7
x2bar = sum(x2) / 7

s11 = sum((x1 - x1bar)^2) / 7
s22 = sum((x2 - x2bar)^2) / 7

s12 = sum((x1 - x1bar) * (x2 - x2bar)) / 7
```

x1bar = 4.285714

x2bar = 6.285714

s11 = 4.204082

s22 = 3.561224

s12 = 3.704082

## 4

```{r}
df = data.frame(
  company = c(
    "Citigroup",
    "General Electric",
    "American Intl Group",
    "Bank of America",
    "HSBC Group",
    "ExxonMobil",
    "Royal Dutch/Shell",
    "BP",
    "ING Group",
    "Toyota Motor"
  ),
  sales = c(108.28, 152.36, 95.04, 65.45, 62.97, 263.99, 265.19, 285.06, 92.01, 165.68),
  profits = c(17.05, 16.59, 10.91, 14.14, 9.52, 25.33, 18.54, 15.73, 8.10, 11.13),
  assets = c(1484.10, 750.33, 766.42, 1110.46, 1031.29,195.26, 193.83, 191.11, 1175.16, 211.15
  )
)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(x = sales, y = profits)) +
  geom_point()
ggplot(df, aes(x = sales)) +
  geom_histogram()
ggplot(df, aes(x = profits)) +
  geom_histogram()

```

There seems to be correlation between sales and profits. This is seen in the linear relationship in the scatter plot.

```{r}

x1 = df$sales
x2 = df$profits

n = length(x1)
x1bar = sum(x1) / n
x2bar = sum(x2) / n

s11 = sum((x1 - x1bar)^2) / n
s22 = sum((x2 - x2bar)^2) / n

s12 = sum((x1 - x1bar) * (x2 - x2bar)) / n

r12 = s12 / sqrt(s11 * s22)
```

x1bar = 155.603

x2bar = 14.704

s11 = 6728.807

s22 = 23.5712

s12 = 273.256

r2 = 0.6861

68.61% of the variation in profits is explained in the variation seen in sales

## 5

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = profits, y = assets)) +
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = sales, y = assets)) +
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
x1 = df$sales
x2 = df$profits
x3 = df$assets

n = length(x1)
x1bar = sum(x1) / n
x2bar = sum(x2) / n
x3bar = sum(x3) / n

xbar = c(x1bar, x2bar, x3bar)
s11 = sum((x1 - x1bar)^2) / n
s22 = sum((x2 - x2bar)^2) / n
s33 = sum((x3 - x3bar)^2) / n

s12 = sum((x1 - x1bar) * (x2 - x2bar)) / n
s13 = sum((x1 - x1bar) * (x3 - x3bar)) / n
s23 = sum((x2 - x2bar) * (x3 - x3bar)) / n

S = matrix(c(
  s11, s12, s13,
  s12, s22, s23,
  s13, s23, s33
), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE)

r12 = s12 / sqrt(s11 * s22)
r13 = s13 / sqrt(s11 * s33)
r23 = s23 / sqrt(s22 * s33)

R = matrix(c(
  1,   r12, r13,
  r12, 1,   r23,
  r13, r23, 1
), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE)


```

```{r}
xbar
S
R
```

## 9

```{r}
x1 = c(-6, -3, -2, 1, 2, 5, 6, 8)
x2 = c(-2, -3, 1, -1, 2, 1, 5, 3)
```

```{r}
prob9 = data.frame(x1 = x1, x2 = x2)
ggplot(prob9, aes(x = x1, y = x2)) +
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
x1 = prob9$x1
x2 = prob9$x2

n = length(x1)
x1bar = sum(x1) / n
x2bar = sum(x2) / n

s11 = sum((x1 - x1bar)^2) / n
s22 = sum((x2 - x2bar)^2) / n

s12 = sum((x1 - x1bar) * (x2 - x2bar)) / n
```

```{r}
s11
s22
s12
```

b\)

```{r}
costheta = .899
sintheta = .438

x1tilde = x1 * costheta + x2 * sintheta
x2tilde = -x1 * sintheta + x2 * costheta
```

```{r}
x1tilde
x2tilde
```

c\)

```{r}
prob9c = data.frame(x1 = x1tilde, x2 = x2tilde)
x1tilde = prob9c$x1
x2tilde = prob9c$x2

ntilde = length(x1tilde)
x1bartilde = sum(x1tilde) / n
x2bartilde = sum(x2tilde) / n

s11tilde = sum((x1tilde - x1bartilde)^2) / n
s22tilde = sum((x2tilde - x2bartilde)^2) / n
```

```{r}
x1bartilde
x2bartilde
s11tilde
s22tilde
```

d\)

```{r}
newpoint_x1 = 4
newpoint_x2 = -2

newpoint_x1tilde = newpoint_x1 * costheta + newpoint_x2 * sintheta
newpoint_x2tilde = -newpoint_x1 * sintheta + newpoint_x2 * costheta

d_to_origin = sqrt(((newpoint_x1tilde^2) / s11tilde) + 
                     ((newpoint_x2tilde^2) / s22tilde))
```

problem d distance to origin: 2.72417

e\)

```{r}

a11 = (costheta^2 / (costheta^2 * s11 + 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2 * s22)) + (sintheta^2 / (costheta^2*s22 - 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2*s11))

a22 = (sintheta^2 / (costheta^2 * s11 + 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2 * s22)) + (costheta^2 / (costheta^2*s22 - 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2*s11))

a12 = ((costheta * sintheta) / (costheta^2 * s11 + 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2 * s22)) - ((sintheta * costheta) / (costheta^2*s22 - 2*sintheta*costheta*s12 + sintheta^2*s11))


d_to_origin_e = sqrt(a11 * newpoint_x1^2 + 
                     2 * a12 * newpoint_x1 * newpoint_x2 + 
                     a22 * newpoint_x2^2)
```

problem e distance to origin: 2.72417, matches with problem d

## 12)

![](images/clipboard-1364593919.png){width="390"}

## 15

```{r}
radiother = read.table("./johnson_wichern_data/T1-7.DAT")
ggplot(radiother, aes(x = V2, y = V3)) +
  geom_point()

```

```{r}
ggplot(radiother, aes(x = V2)) +
  geom_histogram()
ggplot(radiother, aes(x = V3)) +
  geom_histogram()
```

Variable 3 looks to be almost normally distributed but there are many many samples making it slightly right skewed.

b\)

```{r}
compute_means = function(df){
  n = nrow(df)
  p = ncol(df)
  
  xbar = numeric(p)
  for (i in 1:p){
      xbar[i] = sum(df[[i]]) / n
  }
  
  return(xbar)
}

compute_covariance = function(df){
  n = nrow(df)
  p = ncol(df)
  
  col_means = compute_means(df)
  
  S = matrix(0, nrow=p, ncol=p)
  for (i in 1:p){
    for (j in i:p){
      cov_ij = sum((df[[i]] - col_means[i]) * (df[[j]] - col_means[j])) / n
      S[i, j] = cov_ij
      S[j, i] = cov_ij
    }
  }
  return(S)
}

compute_R = function(df){
  p = ncol(df)
  
  S = compute_covariance(df)
  
  R = matrix(0, nrow=p, ncol=p)
  for (i in 1:p){
    for (j in 1:p){
      R[i, j] = S[i, j] / sqrt((S[i, i] * S[j, j]))
    }
  }
  return(R)
}
```

```{r}
compute_means(radiother)
compute_covariance(radiother)
compute_R(radiother)
```

Overall moderate pairwise correlations, however feature 4 (amount of food consumed) and feature 5 (appetite) were most correlated.

## 17

```{r}
library(dplyr)

trackrec = read.table("./johnson_wichern_data/T1-9.dat") %>% select(-V1)

compute_means(trackrec)
compute_covariance(trackrec)
compute_R(trackrec)

```

I notice as you go off of the main diagonal the correlation coefficients drop from .9s to .6s. Similar distance races are much more correlated with each other than races in much different distances.

```{r}
mps_df = trackrec %>% 
        mutate(
          v2_mps = 100 / V2,
          v3_mps = 200 / V3,
          v4_mps = 400 / V4,
          v5_mps = 800 / (V5 * 60),
          v6_mps = 1500 / (V6 * 60),
          v7_mps = 3000 / (V7 * 60),
          v8_mps = 42195 / (V8 * 60)
        ) %>% select(v2_mps, v3_mps, v4_mps, v5_mps, v6_mps, v7_mps, v8_mps)

compute_means(mps_df)
compute_covariance(mps_df)
compute_R(mps_df)
```

Very similar results as 17. As you go off of the main diagonal, the results are less correlated with each other. This indicates races with similar distances are more correlated than races with different distances in both time and velocity.
